channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Spotlight on Carmelle Collado in One Music PH 360
item.description: Carmelle Collado, a rising star in the Philippine music scene, is featured in the latest episode of One Music PH 360. The segment highlights her journey as an artist, showcasing her live performances ...

item.title: Debate on Political Parties Act and Its Impact on Party Autonomy and Democracy
item.description: Stakeholders in Tanzania are raising concerns over the Political Parties Act, 2024, claiming eya restricts political freedom by giving too much power to the Registrar of Political Parties (RPPs). Crit...

item.title: Hudson Young and Ethan Strange Selected for NSW Blues, Presenting Challenges for Raiders Line-up
item.description: Canberra Raiders fans will be cheering as two of their players, Hudson Young and Ethan Strange, earn selection in the NSW Blues squad for the upcoming State of Origin opener at Homebush on Wednesday, ...

item.title: Traffic resumes on Highway 427 off-ramp to Rutherford Road after overnight accident in Vaughan
item.description: The off-ramp from Highway 427 to Rutherford Road in Vaughan has reopened following an overnight crash that temporarily disrupted traffic. Authorities responded promptly to the incident, ensuring that ...

item.title: International divers assist in recovery of Italian victims from Maldives cave
item.description: A multinational team of expert divers has arrived in the Maldives to recover the bodies of four Italian tourists who died while scuba diving near Vaavu Atoll. The tragedy follows the death of Italian ...

item.title: Ottawa weather forecast calls for hot temperatures and periods of rain
item.description: Ottawa residents can expect hot weather accompanied by some rain over the coming days, according to the latest forecast. As the region transitions into the warmer season, temperatures are set to rise ...

item.title: Letters Discuss Provincial Rights and Section 92A of the Constitution
item.description: This article is a collection of letters to the editor discussing the historical and ongoing importance of Section 92A of the Constitution Act, 1982, which affirms provincial jurisdiction over non-rene...

item.title: CNG Prices in Delhi Increased Twice in 48 Hours and Cross Rs 80 per Kg Due to Higher Input Costs
item.description: In a notable development, the price of compressed natural gas or CNG in Delhi has been raised twice within just 48 hours, crossing the Rs 80 per kg mark for the first time. On Sunday, Indraprastha Gas...

item.title: NHMP restricts animal-laden vehicles on motorways to ensure road safety for Eid travel
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The National Highways and Motorway Police (NHMP) have announced a ban on vehicles carrying sacrificial animals from entering motorways, just before Eid ul Adha. According to NHMP officials,...

item.title: West Coast Eagles defeat GWS to end six-game losing streak with standout performances
item.description: West Coast Eagles finally broke their six-game losing streak with a thrilling 17-point victory over Greater Western Sydney at Optus Stadium. The standout performer was 21-year-old Harley Reid, who ama...

item.title: Suba East MP Millie Odhiambo Shows Off Dance Skills at Ramogi Night
item.description: During a lively Ramogi Night event, Suba East MP Millie Odhiambo kept the crowd entertained with her energetic dance moves. Known for her bold personality and nicknamed Bad Gal Millie, she arrived at ...

item.title: Kenya pushes to regulate digital assets and boost blockchain and crypto adoption
item.description: Kenya is making big moves to establish itself as Africa’s leading hub for blockchain and cryptocurrency. Government officials and private sector players are pushing for balanced regulations that will ...

item.title: Solution to Evan Birnholz’s May 17 Crossword Featuring Theater Plays
item.description: Evan Birnholz’s May 17 crossword, titled 'Play Ball,' is a clever puzzle that challenges solvers with a theatrical twist rather than focusing on baseball, as the title might initially suggest. The cro...

item.title: Investor in Anthropic and OpenAI highlights lack of Australian AI companies for investment

item.title: South African Filmmakers Shine at 2026 Cannes Film Festival
item.description: At the 79th Cannes International Film Festival in France, South African filmmakers are making a strong impression, signalling a shift in global cinema with African voices taking centre stage. Cape Tow...

item.title: NHS tribunal ruling could lead to numerous claims over single-sex facilities policy
item.description: A recent Leeds Employment Tribunal ruling has highlighted potential widespread discrimination within the NHS regarding its policies on single-sex facilities. An NHS employee successfully claimed that ...

item.title: New Calcium-Copper-Silicon Clathrate Discovered in Trinitite from 1945 Trinity Nuclear Test
item.description: During the Trinity nuclear test on July 16, 1945, the first detonation of an atomic bomb in New Mexico, extreme conditions led to the spontaneous formation of a novel material never before seen in nat...

item.title: Indian Space-Tech Companies Forge Partnerships with European Firms in Italy
item.description: India’s space promotion and regulatory body, IN-SPACe, led a delegation of nine Indian space-tech companies to participate in the Space Meetings Veneto 2026 in Venice, Italy. The event, organised by A...

item.title: Heavy Rain Causes Flooding in Quezon City
item.description: Quezon City experienced significant flooding after heavy rains swept through various parts of the area. Streets, including Mariveles Street in La Loma, were inundated, causing inconvenience for reside...

item.title: Addressing Canada's Tree Deficit: Strategies for Rebuilding Resilient Forests
item.description: Canada is facing a significant loss of its forest cover, with recent analyses estimating 7.35 billion trees lost due to wildfires, pests, and human activity. The boreal forest, which spans coast to co...
